params.name: Eiffel Tower Blueprints
params.description: A radical fusion of industrial mathematics and Art Nouveau audacity, these 1889 drawings birthed Paris’ iron icon. Gustave Eiffel’s team defied gravity (and critics) with 18,038 interlocking parts—transforming steel into a lattice of pure ambition. More than construction plans, they became the DNA of steampunk fantasy, their geometric poetry still shaping skylines from Vegas to anime cities.
